nodes.context: Armed Chinese robbers and bandits operating in eastern Russia-China borderland, southeastern Siberia, Russian Far East, and Manchuria during late 19th to early 20th century.
nodes.time_period: second half of 19th century - first half of 20th century

nodes.context: Japanese military force that recruited Honghuzi as guerrillas during the Russo-Japanese War.
Chinese anti-foreign, anti-colonial movement; Honghuzi were also Boxers during the Boxer Rebellion.
Coalition of eight nations that fought against the Boxer Rebellion; Honghuzi ambushed their Sikh forces.
Army of Honghuzi led by Zhang Zuolin, allied with Japan during the Russo-Japanese War.
A faction of Honghuzi led by Chung Tsor Lin, later incorporated into the Chinese army.
Chinese troops commanded by General Ma, which included 10,000 Honghuzi wearing imperial uniform.
Temporary bands of peasants who turned to robbery during the kaoliang growing season in Manchuria.
A band of Honghuzi directed by Japanese officers, conducting guerrilla warfare against Russians.

edges.context: Honghuzi were recruited as guerrillas by the Imperial Japanese Army during the Russo-Japanese War.
Honghuzi were also Boxers and participated in the Boxer Rebellion against the Eight-Nation Alliance.
Honghuzi ambushed, tortured, and executed Sikh forces of the Eight-Nation Alliance during the Boxer Rebellion.
Zhang Zuolin's Honghuzi army was allied with Japan and fought against Russians during the Russo-Japanese War.
Zhang Zuolin commanded a large army of Honghuzi bandits.
Chung Tsor Lin's band was a faction of the larger Honghuzi bandit groups.
General Ma's troops included 10,000 Honghuzi who were adopted into the Chinese army.
Peasant bands sometimes fought the Honghuzi or joined them temporarily.
A band of Honghuzi was directed by Japanese officers.
